title: Computations of Heegaard Floer Homologies
speaker: András Stipsicz
affiliation: Renyi Institute of Mathematics, Hungarian Academy of Sciences
abstract: Heegaard Floer homology groups were recently introduced by Ozsvath and Szabo to study properties of 3-manifolds and knots in them. The definition of the invariants rests on delicate holomorphic geometry, making the actual computations cumbersome. In the lecture we will recall the basic definitions and theorems of the theory, and show how to define the simplest version in a purely combinatorial manner. For a special class of 3-manifolds the more general version will be presented by simple combinatorial ideas through lattice homology of Nemethi.

proof_stages.label: Motivation and variants of Heegaard Floer theory
proof_stages.summary: The talk introduces HF-hat and HF-minus, their Spin-c and grading refinements, knot and link versions, and the topological applications that motivate making the theory computable.

proof_stages.label: Heegaard diagrams as presentations of three-manifolds
proof_stages.summary: A closed oriented three-manifold is encoded by a surface, alpha and beta curves, and basepoints; the resulting handle construction gives every three-manifold, and isotopies, handle slides, and stabilizations relate different presentations.

proof_stages.label: Analytic Heegaard Floer chain complex
proof_stages.summary: From a Heegaard diagram the symmetric product contains alpha and beta tori whose intersection points generate the complex, while the differential counts holomorphic disks with basepoint multiplicities, yielding HF-minus and the simpler HF-hat specialization.

proof_stages.label: Computability program
proof_stages.summary: The speaker formulates the computational problem as replacing hard holomorphic-disk counts first by a combinatorial scheme, then by an invariant combinatorial definition, and ultimately by an efficient one.

proof_stages.label: Nice diagrams and combinatorial HF-hat
proof_stages.summary: Sarkar-Wang nice diagrams reduce the HF-hat differential to counts of bigons and rectangles, and joint work proves that the resulting combinatorial complex is invariant under suitable diagram moves, though often computationally large.

proof_stages.label: Lattice homology for plumbed three-manifolds
proof_stages.summary: Because the nice-diagram strategy does not handle HF-minus, the talk restricts to weighted-tree plumbings and defines a purely combinatorial infinite chain complex from characteristic cohomology classes, vertex subsets, and graph-theoretic weights.

proof_stages.label: Comparison theorems for lattice and Heegaard Floer homology
proof_stages.summary: Nemethi's theorem identifies lattice homology with HF-minus for negative definite trees with at most one bad vertex, and recent work extends the comparison via a spectral sequence that collapses in broader bad-vertex ranges.

proof_stages.label: Knot filtration and induction toward the negative definite case
proof_stages.summary: An unweighted extra vertex induces a filtration analogous to knot Floer homology, enabling surgery computations, plumbing-graph invariance, and an inductive work-in-progress proof that lattice homology agrees with HF-minus for negative definite graphs.
